subject: Science
chapter_name: The World of Metals and Non-Metals
overall_learning_objectives: Compare physical properties of metals and non-metals with scientific terminology
Understand the chemical property of reaction with Oxygen and Water
Identify the nature of metallic and non-metallic oxides (Acidic/Basic)
Explain the displacement reaction using the reactivity series concept
Recognize exceptions in physical properties (Mercury, Sodium, Graphite)
Understand the process and prevention of Galvanization and Alloying
Correlate the properties of materials with their industrial applications

lesson_plan.title: Physical Properties & The Exceptions
topics_covered: Luster, Malleability, Ductility, and Sonority (Advanced definitions)
Non-metal exceptions: Lustrous Iodine and Conductive Graphite
student_activities: Test the conductivity of a pencil lead (Graphite)

lesson_plan.title: Reaction with Oxygen
topics_covered: Nature of oxides (Basic for metals, Acidic for non-metals)
teaching_strategy: Litmus test of the resulting ash ($MgO$)
Word equations: $Metal + Oxygen ightarrow Metal\ Oxide$
student_activities: Observe color change in litmus when testing magnesium ash
Write word equations for Iron and Copper oxidation

lesson_plan.title: Reaction with Water and Acids
topics_covered: Vigorous reaction of Sodium with water
Reaction of metals with dilute acids to produce Hydrogen gas
teaching_strategy: Video demonstration of Sodium in water (Safety first)
Instruction on safety while handling acids
student_activities: Perform the 'Pop' sound test under supervision
Compare the reactivity of Iron, Zinc, and Copper with acid
assessment: Short answer test on Hydrogen production

lesson_plan.title: Displacement Reactions
topics_covered: Reaction: $Iron + Copper\ Sulphate ightarrow Iron\ Sulphate + Copper$
Why a 'stronger' metal pushes out a 'weaker' one
teaching_strategy: Experiment: Iron nails in Blue Vitriol (Copper Sulphate) solution
student_activities: Monitor the color change of the solution over 30 minutes
Predict outcomes of different metal-salt combinations

lesson_plan.title: Corrosion and Prevention
teaching_strategy: Display of galvanized vs. non-galvanized steel
student_activities: Set up the rusting experiment for observation over 2 days
Discuss why ships rust faster in seawater

lesson_plan.title: Alloys and Uses of Non-Metals
topics_covered: Uses of Sulfur (crackers), Phosphorus (matches), and Nitrogen (fertilizers)
teaching_strategy: Show and tell with different alloy samples
Discussion on why pure gold is not used for jewelry
student_activities: Match the alloy to its constituent metals
List 5 non-metals used in agriculture and medicine
assessment: Group presentation on 'The Importance of Non-Metals'

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Mastery Assessment
student_activities: Participate in a team-based science quiz
assessment: Detailed MCQ section including 'Reasoning and Assertion'

teaching_aids: Magnesium ribbon, Zinc granules, Iron nails
Samples of Brass, Bronze, and Stainless Steel
Testing tubes, spirit lamps, and litmus paper
teacher_tip: Students often struggle with the 'Displacement Reaction' concept. Use the 'Bully vs. Weakling' analogy to explain how a more reactive metal takes the place of a less reactive one. Always emphasize the color changes as they are the best visual evidence for Class 7.
